questions.question: What is reflection of light?
questions.explanation: Reflection is the bouncing back of light from a surface into the same medium.

questions.question: The angle between the incident ray and the normal is called:
questions.explanation: The angle of incidence is the angle between the incident ray and the normal.

questions.question: According to the laws of reflection, the angle of incidence is:
questions.explanation: The first law of reflection states that angle of incidence equals angle of reflection.

questions.question: Which type of mirror has a flat reflecting surface?
questions.explanation: A plane mirror has a flat surface and produces a virtual image.

questions.question: The image formed by a plane mirror is:
questions.explanation: Plane mirrors produce virtual and erect images of the same size.

questions.question: What is refraction of light?

options.C: Bending of light when passing between media

questions.explanation: Refraction occurs when light changes direction due to change in speed in different media.

questions.question: Refraction occurs because:

options.B: Speed of light changes in different media

questions.explanation: The change in speed of light in different media causes refraction.

questions.question: The refractive index of a medium indicates:

options.B: Speed of light in that medium

questions.explanation: Refractive index relates to how much the speed of light decreases in a medium.

questions.question: When light travels from a rarer medium to a denser medium, it bends:
questions.explanation: Light bends toward the normal when entering a denser medium.

questions.question: When light travels from a denser medium to a rarer medium, it bends:
questions.explanation: Light bends away from the normal when moving to a rarer medium.

questions.question: Which device is used to study refraction in laboratories?
questions.explanation: A glass slab is commonly used to demonstrate refraction of light.

questions.question: Which of the following represents a normal line in reflection diagrams?
questions.explanation: The normal is an imaginary line perpendicular to the reflecting surface.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a law of reflection?

options.A: Angle of incidence equals angle of reflection
options.B: Incident ray and reflected ray lie in the same plane
options.D: Normal lies in the same plane

questions.explanation: Bending of light refers to refraction, not reflection.

questions.question: Which type of image cannot be obtained on a screen?
questions.explanation: Virtual images cannot be projected onto a screen.

questions.question: The bending of light in water makes objects appear:
questions.explanation: Due to refraction, objects under water appear closer to the surface.

questions.question: The speed of light is maximum in:
questions.explanation: Light travels fastest in vacuum compared to other media.

questions.question: Which phenomenon explains why a straw appears bent in water?
questions.explanation: The bending of light at the water-air boundary causes this effect.

questions.question: Which of the following mediums is optically denser?
questions.explanation: Glass has a higher refractive index than air or water.

questions.question: Which of the following best explains lateral inversion?

options.A: Left and right sides appear reversed

questions.explanation: Plane mirrors reverse the left and right sides of the image.

questions.question: Which property of light explains mirror reflection?
questions.explanation: Light travels in straight lines, enabling predictable reflection patterns.

questions.question: Which instrument uses reflection of light?
questions.explanation: Periscopes use mirrors to reflect light and view objects from hidden positions.

questions.question: Which surface produces regular reflection?
questions.explanation: Smooth surfaces like mirrors produce regular reflection.

questions.question: Which phenomenon allows lenses to form images?
questions.explanation: Lenses work by bending light through refraction.

questions.question: Which device commonly uses refraction?
questions.explanation: Lenses use refraction to focus or spread light rays.

questions.question: Which of the following describes light behavior correctly?

options.A: Light travels in curved paths always
options.B: Light travels in straight lines in a uniform medium

questions.explanation: In a uniform medium, light travels in straight lines.
